free_text: Cross sections of alpha-particle induced reactions on
High purity metalic Ni foils (5um) with natural
isotopic composition and Ti for beam monitoring (5 and
consisting of Ni-Ni and Ti-Ti foil pairs were
irradiated with alpha beam of E=51.04, 49.42, 40.94 and
26.57 MeV. The beam energy was determined by TOF
method. Irradiation was done in a Faraday-cup like
targetholder. Beam instensity correction based on
applied for experiment1 to experiment4 respectively.
The beam intensity was monitored and registered in
Several series of spectra were recorded with different
cooling times to be able to follow the decay.
Decay data were taken from NUDAT2, Lund/LBNL and
IAEA-NDS Live Chart online data libraries.
The uncertainty on the median energy in the
foils includes the cumulative effects of uncertainty of
incident beam energy, uncertainty of energy loss of
each foil due to thickness variation and straggling
effects. Uncertainty of the primary incident beam
energy was +- 0.2MeV which increased up to +- 0.9 MeV
in the last Ni foil of the stack.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
contributing parameters. Uncertainty of time parameters
